steps.title: Use slope direction and intercept
steps.content: The displayed best-fit line slopes downward, so the slope must be negative. It also crosses the $y$-axis near $2.3$, not near $16.3$.

steps.title: Reject the positive-slope choices
steps.content: Start by graphing choices A and C. Both have positive slope, so they rise from left to right. The displayed best-fit line falls from left to right, so A and C cannot match.

steps.title: Reject the high-intercept choice
steps.content: Now test choice B, $y=-x+16.3$. Its slope is negative, but its $y$-intercept is $16.3$, far above the displayed intercept near $2.3$. B is out.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The line $y=-x+2.3$ has the correct downward direction and $y$-intercept. Choice D is correct.
